bull_thesis: Green Dot is in a binding, definitive take-private/restructuring: CommerceOne acquires GDOT and combines Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company (NewCo), while Smith Ventures buys the fintech/embedded-finance business for $690M cash. Per-share consideration is $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares, which management implies is worth $14.23 (low TBV multiple) to $19.18 (high) at close. The shareholder vote is 2026-06-23 (imminent, board-recommended, no competing bid or activist no-vote campaign), and close is expected by end of Q2-2026. Live price ~$12.80 sits BELOW even the low-end $14.23 resolved value, so the deal closing alone delivers ~+11% gross in days-to-weeks, with optionality to +50% ($19.18) if NewCo re-rates to the upper TBV multiple. The cash leg ($8.11, 63% of the low-end value) is contractually fixed; standalone fundamentals are healthy (Q1-26 rev +17% YoY, NI +109%, EPS beat $0.30), so even a break reverts to recent unaffected levels rather than a distressed mark. Conditions hold if: the vote passes (near-certain) and the Fed/OCC bank-holding-company formation approval clears on the Q2 timeline.
bear_thesis: The consideration is only 78% cash; ~22% is 0.2215 shares of an UNLISTED, newly-formed NewCo bank holding company whose realized tangible-book multiple, post-close liquidity, and combined credit/deposit quality are unknown — so the 'value' could land at the $14.23 low end (or below if NewCo trades poorly out of the gate), not the $19.18 headline. The genuine residual risk is the bank-regulatory gate: forming a new BHC requires Federal Reserve / OCC / state approval, which can slip the timeline past Q2 (SOFT-extension risk) or, in the tail, fail. Three NY strike-suits (Phillips, Richardson, Zaccagnino) and multiple firm investigations create deal-tax noise. If the deal breaks, GDOT reverts toward the unaffected/52-wk-low zone (~$11.55-$11.80, tail to $9.31), a -8% to worse outcome. The standalone franchise is a commoditized, payments-disintermediation-exposed prepaid/BaaS issuer with no durable moat, so there is no compounding safety net if the catalyst evaporates.
sop_bull: Win value = full deal consideration realized toward management's range. $8.11 cash + 0.2215 x NewCo. At the high-end TBV multiple management cites, blended ~$19.18/share (+50% vs live $12.80). Base-win (low-end of management's own range, conservative TBV mark) = $14.23/share (+11%). Bull SoP anchor: $16.50-$19.18 if NewCo re-rates; conservative win = $14.23.
sop_bear: Verified downside floor = deal-break / standalone reversion. GDOT loses the stock-component optionality and reverts to recent unaffected trading near the 50-day low / pre-spread zone ~$11.55-$11.80 (52-wk low $9.31 is the distressed tail, unlikely given stable Q1-26 fundamentals). Working bear floor: ~$11.80 (-7.8% vs live $12.80). The hard cash leg ($8.11) only floors the consideration if the deal closes; on a break it is the standalone equity, not $8.11, that sets the mark.
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case. Assign ~88% to deal close at the conservative low-end value $14.23 (vote 06-23 near-certain; bank-regulatory approval the only real gate, no slippage announced) and ~12% to a break reverting to the ~$11.80 floor: 0.88*14.23 + 0.12*11.80 = $13.94, round to ~$13.95. This excludes the $19.18 high-end NewCo re-rate optionality (treated as free upside, not booked in base). Components: $8.11 hard cash + 0.2215 NewCo stock leg marked conservatively at the low-end TBV multiple = $14.23 win; $11.80 break floor.
risk_reward: Upside-to-target +11.2% ($12.80 -> $14.23 low-end) vs downside-to-floor -7.8% ($12.80 -> $11.80) = ~1.4:1 to the conservative target, rising to 3-6:1 if NewCo re-rates toward the $19.18 high-end. Asymmetry is favorable and the catalyst is days away.
catalyst_summary: Binding definitive merger: CommerceOne acquires GDOT + Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company; Smith Ventures buys the fintech business for $690M cash. Consideration $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares (mgmt-implied $14.23-$19.18). DEFM14A filed 2026-05-08; shareholder special meetings 2026-06-23; expected close Q2-2026 (by ~06-30) pending Fed/OCC/state bank-regulatory approval. Live $12.80 sits below the $14.23 low-end resolved value — a genuine PENDING_HARD arb discount, not trading through terms. Strike-suits already mooted via supplemental disclosures.
dated_milestone: Shareholder vote (GDOT + CommerceOne special meetings) 2026-06-23; expected deal close in Q2-2026 (by ~2026-06-30) subject to final bank-regulatory approval.
forcing_function: The 2026-06-23 shareholder vote (binary, board-recommended, no competing bid) followed by the bank-regulatory close in Q2-2026 forces the spread to collapse to the deal value. Vote pass -> close -> $8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo distributed; that is the hard trigger that re-rates the stub from $12.80 to >=$14.23.
consensus_delta: The sell-side carries a 'Hold' rating with a ~$14.75 consensus price target and treats GDOT as an ambiguous standalone payments name (range $14.25-$18 PT). That framing is stale: the company is no longer a going-concern compounding story — it is a near-certain, dated deal-completion with a vote 24 hours out. The market is leaving an ~11% spread to the low-end deal value ($14.23) and ignoring the embedded NewCo re-rate optionality to $19.18, because the stock-component and the unlisted-NewCo mark scare generalist holders into discounting the whole consideration to roughly the cash leg plus a thin premium. The expectations error: pricing residual deal-break/regulatory risk at ~12% when the vote is board-recommended, uncontested, the only real gate is a routine (if slip-prone) BHC formation approval, and the strike-suits are already mooted by supplemental disclosures.
valley_of_death: Near-term (next 1-9 weeks) the only genuine hazard is timeline slip on the Fed/OCC/state bank-holding-company formation approval pushing close past Q2-2026 (a SOFT-extension that would carry the spread but delay the cash). No cash-burn hump, no debt-maturity wall, and standalone FCF/earnings are healthy (Q1-26 NI +109% YoY), so there is no fundamental decay that punishes the stub before the catalyst. The disclosure strike-suits (Phillips/Richardson/Zaccagnino) are deal-tax noise — no injunction sought, claims mooted via supplemental disclosures — and do not threaten the close. The real tail is a regulatory denial of the new BHC, which would break the deal and revert GDOT to ~$11.80 (-8%).
positioning_washout: Already largely de-risked: arb desks hold the spread, and the remaining generalist/passive base faces a clean cash-plus-stock close rather than a dividend cut or pivot that forces mechanical liquidation. The one mechanical event is that holders who do not want unlisted NewCo paper (index funds, certain mandates that cannot hold the new BHC) will sell into the close, which partly explains why the spread to $14.23 persists — that selling pressure is the source of the discount, not a sign the deal is at risk. Post-close, NewCo's initial trading could see forced selling from holders who only wanted GDOT, a separate (and tradable) dynamic from the arb itself.
moderator_conclusion: GDOT is a textbook held-seat deal-completion arb at the optimal entry point. The catalyst is PENDING_HARD: a binding definitive merger ($8.11 cash + 0.2215 NewCo shares, mgmt-implied $14.23-$19.18), a board-recommended shareholder vote 2026-06-23 (tomorrow), and an expected Q2-2026 close, with the live price ~$12.80 sitting BELOW even the conservative low-end deal value of $14.23. That is precisely the 5/A configuration — dated/binding catalyst inside the window, price well below the event-resolved target, and a defined ~$11.80 break floor that bounds the loss to ~-8% against +11% to the low-end target and up to +50% to the $19.18 high-end. Do NOT cap this like a priced compounder: the moat is NONE and the standalone is payments-disintermediation-exposed, but that is by design — the catalyst carries the thesis. The one real risk is the bank-regulatory BHC-formation gate slipping the timeline (carries the spread, doesn't kill it) plus the unlisted-NewCo stock-component mark, which is why I set sop_fair_value at $13.95 (88/12 weighted) rather than booking the high-end. value_conviction is 3, not 5: stripped of the catalyst this is an optically-fair, no-moat franchise — the asymmetry is entirely event-sourced. Verdict A, conviction 5 on the event asymmetry; HELD seat affirmed, catalyst imminent.
interrogator_dossier:
# GDOT — Green Dot Corp — Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven / Merger-Arb)

**Frame:** This is NOT a business-quality or compounding judgment. Green Dot is a structurally-pressured prepaid-card / BaaS issuer with a thin, eroding standalone franchise. The thesis is the SPREAD between the live price (~$12.80, 2026-06-22) and the event-resolved deal value, gated on the merger firing, with a defined cash floor that bounds the loss. The catalyst carries the thesis.

## 1. THE EVENT — is it real, dated, binding?

**Real and binding.** On 2026-11-23 Green Dot announced definitive agreements (Merger Agreement + Separation Agreement) under which: CommerceOne Financial Corp. acquires Green Dot and combines Green Dot Bank into a new publicly-traded bank holding company (NewCo); Smith Ventures LLC simultaneously acquires the fintech/embedded-finance business from NewCo for $690M cash. Per-share consideration: $8.11 cash + 0.2215 shares of NewCo. Former GDOT holders own ~72% of NewCo, CommerceOne ~28%. Aggregate value $825M-$1.1B (incl. ~$470M cash). Management-stated implied per-share value range: $14.23 (low TBV multiple) - $19.18 (high) at close.

**Dated and imminent.** DEFM14A filed 2026-05-08. Special meeting / shareholder vote 2026-06-23 (tomorrow). Expected close Q2-2026 (by ~06-30), contingent on final regulatory sign-off.

**Conditions:** shareholder approval (both special meetings 06-23), required bank regulatory approvals (Fed/OCC/state — a BHC formation, so the regulatory step is the genuine residual risk, not antitrust), and customary closing conditions.

## 2. THE VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR

The floor is NOT the $8.11 cash alone, because ~22% of consideration is unlisted NewCo stock. Cash leg $8.11 is contractually fixed/hard. Stock leg 0.2215 x NewCo marked conservatively floors the blended DEAL value at management's low-end $14.23. A break floor (deal fails, GDOT reverts standalone) sits near the recent unaffected / 50-day-low zone ~$11.55-$11.80 (52-wk low $9.31 is the tail). Working downside floor for the held seat: ~$11.80 — deal-break reversion, comfortably below live $12.80 and well below the $14.23 low-end deal value. Bounds loss at roughly -8% vs upside-to-low-end +11% ($14.23) and up to +50% ($19.18) on a high-end NewCo re-rate.

## 3. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

RED — Stock-component risk: ~22% of consideration is 0.2215 shares of an unlisted NewCo BHC; post-close liquidity, realized TBV multiple, and combined credit/deposit profile unknown. The 'target' is the LOW end of management's own range; do not credit $19.18 as base.
RED — Bank-regulatory gate: requires Fed/OCC/state approval to FORM a new BHC; the one condition that can slip past Q2 (SOFT-extension risk), though no slippage announced.
YELLOW — Strike-suit overhang (benign): three NY disclosure-deficiency suits (Phillips 05-27, Richardson 05-28, Zaccagnino 06-08) + Schall/Wohl & Fruchter/Kaskela investigations. Company already issued supplemental merger disclosures — textbook moot-the-claim pattern; no injunction sought/granted.
GREEN — No financing condition of note on the cash leg; binding gate is regulatory + vote, both imminent.
GREEN — Standalone fundamentals stable not collapsing: Q1-26 rev +17% YoY to $656.2M, NI +109% YoY to $53.7M, EPS beat $0.30 — supports the break-floor near unaffected levels.
GREEN — Trading BELOW low-end deal value, not through terms: live $12.80 < $14.23 — a genuine arb discount (PENDING_HARD), not an ARB name at/through the offer.

## 4. SPREAD ECONOMICS

Live $12.80 (2026-06-22, intraday $12.76-$12.92). Target (low-end deal value, base) $14.23 -> +11.2% gross, captured on close. Floor (break/standalone) ~$11.80 -> -7.8%. Asymmetry ~1.4-1.6:1 to low-end target; 3-6:1 if NewCo re-rates toward $19.18. Catalyst PENDING_HARD: dated (vote 06-23, close Q2-26), binding (definitive agreements), real asymmetry, defined floor.

## 5. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

EVENT-DRIVEN / DEAL-COMPLETION, not a franchise judgment. Single most important thing to weigh: the stock-component + bank-regulatory tail — the $8.11 cash leg is hard, but ~22% of value rides on an unlisted NewCo whose mark sets whether realized value lands at $14.23 or $19.18, and the Fed/OCC formation approval is the one gate that could slip. The 06-23 vote is near-certain (board-recommended, no competing bid/activist no-vote); strike-suits are mooted noise. Clean held-seat arb, catalyst imminent, live price below low-end resolved value. Moat NONE (commoditized prepaid/BaaS, payments-disintermediation-exposed) but irrelevant; the catalyst carries the thesis. Secular threat manageable for the event value. Trajectory STABLE.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
